channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Investigators say Trump assassination suspect shot officer at press gala
item.description: Secret Service agent at White House correspondents' dinner was 'definitively' hit by suspect's bullet, prosecutor says.

item.title: Floods and landslides kill at least 18 in Kenya
item.description: Ongoing rains are causing floods and landslides in the East African nation.

item.title: Man United beat Liverpool 3-2 to secure Champions League qualification
item.description: Kobbie Mainoo's late strike earns a thrilling victory after bitter rivals Liverpool had fought back from two goals down.

item.title: Bulk carrier attacked by multiple small craft off Iran, UKMTO says
item.description: Crew is safe and no environmental impact has been reported after the incident 11 nautical miles (20km) west of Sirik.

item.title: The US vs Latin America: Varsha Gandikota and Susana Muhamad
item.description: Gandikota asks Muhamad: What does the military‑industrial machine mean for the sovereignty of the Global South?

item.title: Israel threatens Gaza war resumption to force disarmament as ‘truce’ frays
item.description: Palestinian factions reject US-backed plans linking aid to weapons surrender, demanding clear political path forward.

item.title: Zelenskyy has no cards to play against Russia or the West
item.description: The president seems unable to secure for Ukrainian people a gain that can compensate for a painful peace.

item.title: On World Press Freedom Day, Pope honours journalists killed in war zones
item.description: Pope Leo XIV asks faithful to remember journalists who have lost their lives pursuing the truth.

item.title: Who are the two Gaza flotilla activists abducted by Israel?
item.description: Spanish national Saif Abu Keshek and Brazilian Thiago Avila were among 175 Gaza flotilla activists detained by Israel.

item.title: US to cut troops in Germany as Iran war spat deepens
item.description: US-German relations are straining as a spat deepens between President Trump and Chancellor Merz over the war on Iran.

item.title: USA vs Paraguay among World Cup games with unsold, exorbitant tickets
item.description: Tickets for the cohosts' opening game in Los Angeles are available for prices ranging between $1,120 and $6,050.

item.title: It does not make sense to invest more Arab resources in a US alliance
item.description: The US will always prioritise Israel over the Arab world. Arab unity is the only way to establish security for all.

item.title: Netanyahu boasts of Israel’s billion-dollar military investments
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu says Israel will invest $119 billion USD into developing its own military weapons systems.

item.title: Was the Iran war the final blow in the collapse of Spirit Airlines?
item.description: The shutdown of the budget airline after 34 years has left 17,000 staff members unemployed.

item.title: OPEC+ announces symbolic oil output rise during Strait of Hormuz closure
item.description: Seven major producers will add 188,000 barrels per day to their total production quota for June, OPEC+ says.

item.title: Israel approves purchase of fighter jets from the US
item.description: Purchase includes two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing.

item.title: US military says two service members taking part in Morocco drills missing
item.description: AFRICOM says search and rescue operations under way in southwestern Morocco during African Lion exercise.

item.title: Taiwan’s leader visits Eswatini despite pressure from China to block trip
item.description: Taiwan’s leader William Lai Ching-te is visiting Eswatini, the only country in Africa that recognises Taiwan.

item.title: Two women die in attempted Channel crossing from France to UK
item.description: Women die after boat carrying 82 people has engine trouble and runs aground near Calais, French authorities say.

item.title: Gaza aid flotilla activists appear in Israeli court after abduction
item.description: Court extends activists’ detention by two days for further interrogation, rights group tells Al Jazeera.

item.title: Manchester United vs Liverpool 3-2: Premier League – as it happened
item.description: All the updates as United beat rivals Liverpool at Old Trafford to secure Champions League football next season.

item.title: Who is Cherie DeVaux, the first female trainer to win the Kentucky Derby?
item.description: DeVaux-trained Golden Tempo beats the odds to give jockey Jose Ortiz a win in the derby for the first time.

item.title: What’s Iran’s 14-point proposal to end the war? And will Trump accept it?
item.description: Trump is reviewing Iran's latest proposal to end the war, but the mistrust between the two sides is a big obstacle.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz blockade and other major naval sieges in modern times
item.description: Naval blockades are among the oldest weapons of war, using sea control to cut supplies and force an enemy’s surrender.

item.title: Hospitals in Lebanon overwhelmed with patients injured by Israeli attacks
item.description: Hospitals in Lebanon overwhelmed with patients injured by Israeli attacks
